name: Arena POWERSKIN VELOCE
description: Arena's new racer runs on Hyperforce, their first tense-elastic fabric, chasing a fit swimmers actually want: compression without the shrink-wrap feeling. Early word is it moves like a training suit and squeezes like a tech suit, a combination that usually costs you one or the other.

name: Arena Cobra Ultra Swipe goggles
description: fog mid-swim, wipe the lens with a finger, done. Anti-fog that resets itself instead of quitting on you at 50 meters in.

name: FORM Smart Swim 2
description: the HeadCoach algorithm got sharper this year. It'll actually catch you riding too high on freestyle.

name: THEMAGIC5 Vector
description: scan your face from your couch, a robot cuts your gasket. Custom-fit goggles, no fitting room required.

name: FINIS Swimmer's Snorkel
description: the default center-mount snorkel on every pool deck. Doesn't leak, stays in place, and lets you fix your stroke instead of fighting for air.

name: FINIS Cardio Cap Snorkel
description: a restrictor cap that turns the Swimmer's Snorkel into a lung workout. Up to 40% more work on your breathing without changing a single set.

name: Arena Swim Snorkel III
description: the tank of the category. Survives seasons of chlorine abuse, seals leak-free, and the liquid-silicone strap stays comfortable for the long haul.

name: MP Michael Phelps Focus Snorkel
description: low-profile, hydrodynamic, and rock-stable even at full speed with fins. Built for swimmers chasing a perfectly quiet head position.

name: Speedo Center-Mount Snorkel
description: the low-risk way into snorkel training. Comfortable head mount, does the core job for half the price of the premium picks.
